name: thisstuffiswaytootech Source Capture Checklist
purpose: Machine-readable checklist for deciding when future agents should preserve public-safe technical source trails.
addSourceTrailWhen: A future agent would probably need to search the same official documentation again.
The finding controls or clarifies public product, API, SDK, CLI, platform, standards, workflow, or hosting behavior.
The source trail is reusable beyond the current private task.
The note can be written from public first-party sources without exposing private operations.
skipSourceTrailWhen: The source is incidental and not likely to be reused.
The finding depends on private implementation details that cannot be safely summarized.
The only available support is a forum answer, social post, search snippet, or AI-generated claim that is not backed by a controlling public source.
requiredDocSections: What These Sources Do Not Prove
jsonUpdateSteps: Add or update a unique officialSources[].id in data/source-index.json.
Use an existing sourceType from sourceTypes[].id.
Include capturedAt, urls, supports, and doesNotProve.
Add the doc path to relatedFiles in data/source-index.json.
Add the source ID to an existing topic starterSources array in data/topic-manifest.json, or create a topic only when no existing topic owns the scope.
Update dateModified in changed JSON files.

sourceRecordTemplate.title: Human-Readable Source Trail Title
supports: Public technical claim the official source supports.
doesNotProve: Private deployment, account, entitlement, or implementation claim not proven by the public source.

topicUpdateTemplate.label: Human-Readable Topic Label
topicUpdateTemplate.publicScope: Public-safe scope for official docs, source links, and reusable gotchas.
